messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Treat executable checks as the authority.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). If state.cooldown is greater than 0, decrement state.cooldown by 1 and return false. Otherwise set state.cooldown to input.cooldown, increment state.actions by 1, and return true.

meta.repair_reason: Fresh v7 adapter returned true but failed to set state.cooldown when the action fired.
meta.expected_behavior: When cooldown is zero, set cooldown to input.cooldown and increment actions. When cooldown is positive, decrement and return false.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Treat strings as atomic values unless explicitly asked to iterate characters.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). If every string in input.required is present in state.flags, add input.reward to state.rewards if it is not already present, then return true. If any required flag is missing, do not change state.rewards and return false.

meta.repair_reason: BASE split reward strings into characters and fresh v7 failed to add the reward. The reward is one atomic string.
meta.expected_behavior: Only mutate rewards after all required flags are present. Push input.reward as one string, not as characters.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Keep state unchanged when the prompt says do not mutate state.

Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). input.options is an array of objects with id and weight. Return the id of the first option where the cumulative weight is greater than input.roll. If no option crosses the roll, return the final option id. Do not mutate state.

meta.repair_reason: BASE and fresh v7 returned -1 instead of the selected option id.
meta.expected_behavior: Accumulate weights in order, return the first id whose cumulative weight exceeds roll, fallback to final id.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Mutate only the fields required by the prompt.
Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). It must add input.points to state.score, mutate state.score, and return the new score.

meta.repair_reason: Anchor passing behavior so repair training does not forget simple arithmetic mutation.
meta.expected_behavior: Mutate state.score by adding input.points and return the new score.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Preserve existing arrays unless the prompt says to append.
Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). It must increase state.hp by input.heal, cap hp at state.maxHp, push 'healed' into state.events, and return state.hp.

meta.repair_reason: Anchor passing behavior so repair training does not forget clamped mutation plus event append.
meta.expected_behavior: Increase hp, clamp to maxHp, append healed event, return hp.

messages.content: You are a coding agent. Return only TypeScript code. Define exactly function evaluate(input, state). Preserve uniqueness checks.
Write TypeScript only. Define function evaluate(input, state). If input.item is not already in state.inventory, append it. Return the inventory length.

meta.repair_reason: Anchor passing behavior so repair training does not forget uniqueness-preserving array updates.
meta.expected_behavior: Append only missing items and return inventory length.
